GB 17896一2022
3 Terms and definitions
For the purpose of this document，the terms and definitions given in GB/T 32483.1,GB/T 32483.2
and the following apply.
3.1
ballast efficiency
7b
ratio of the output power to the lamp(s) and the total input power of the ballast-lamp circuit
NOTE ”Loads from sensors, network connections and other auxiliary are disconnected or, if not possible,otherwise,， e-
liminated from the result.
3.2
minimum allowable value of energy efficiency for ballast
the minimum allowable value of ballast efficiency under the specified testing conditions
3.3
standby power
average power consumption of a ballast in the standby mode
NOTE 1 Standby mode of ballast is a mode when the ballast is connected to a supply voltage with the ilumination func-
tion of，while capable of being activated by an external trigger not being a trigger from a network.
NOTE 2 ”Examples of external triggers are sensing or timing.
4 Technical requirements
4.1 Energy efficiency grades
4.1.1 The energy efficiency levels of ballasts are classified into three grades,， of which Grade 1 is
the most efficient.
4.1.2 The efficiency of electronic ballasts for tubular fluorescent lamps of each grade shall not be
less than respective values specified in Table 1, and the efficiency of electromagnetic ballasts for tu-
bular fluorescent lamps of each grade shall not be less than respective values specified in Table 2.
2
